input.prompt:
I have a solution with 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects that currently run on VSTest.
I want to switch all of them to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. We're on .NET 8 SDK
and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI. What changes do I need to make to each
project and to the CI command? I want properties set consistently across all projects.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a solution with 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects that currently run on VSTest.
I want to switch all of them to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. We're on .NET 8 SDK
and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI. What changes do I need to make to each
project and to the CI command? I want properties set consistently across all projects.

data.content:
Here's what you need:

## 1. Add a `Directory.Build.props` (at solution root)

This sets properties consistently across all test projects:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <UseTestingPlatform>true</UseTestingPlatform>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

- **`UseTestingPlatform`** — switches MSTest 3.8+ to build as a Microsoft.Testing.Platform executable instead of a VSTest adapter.
- **`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`** — enables the `dotnet test` compatibility layer (required on .NET 8 SDK; the older SDK doesn't natively speak MTP protocol).

## 2. Each test project `.csproj`

No per-project changes needed if `Directory.Build.props` covers them. Just confirm each project already has:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
```

The `MSTest` meta-package (3.8+) already bundles the MTP hosting pieces when `UseTestingPlatform` is set.

## 3. CI command change

```bash
# Before
dotnet test --logger trx

# After
dotnet test --logger trx --results-directory ./TestResults
```

The command itself stays the same — `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` makes `dotnet test` work as before. The `--logger trx` flag is translated automatically.

If you want to call the test executables directly (bypassing `dotnet test`), the equivalent is:

```bash
dotnet run --project <TestProj> -- --report-trx --results-directory ./TestResults
```

## Summary of moving parts

| Concern | Mechanism |
|---------|-----------|
| Switch runtime to MTP | `UseTestingPlatform=true` |
| Keep `dotnet test` working on .NET 8 SDK | `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport=true` |
| TRX output | `--logger trx` still works via the compat layer |
| Consistent across projects | `Directory.Build.props` at solution root |

No other package additions or removals are needed with MSTest 3.8.0 — the runner is already included.
data.reasoningText:
The user wants to migrate 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP). They're on .NET 8 SDK and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI.

Let me provide a concise answer covering the necessary changes.
